official_sources.title: Query | REST API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: GET /services/data/vXX.X/query?q=<SOQL> with Authorization: Bearer; q space encoding (+ or %20); response fields totalSize, done, records and nextRecordsUrl (e.g. /services/data/v67.0/query/01gRO0000016PIAYA2-500); per-record attributes {type, url}; default batches up to 2,000 records; fetching the remainder by requesting nextRecordsUrl until done

official_sources.title: Status Codes and Error Responses | REST API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: error responses as a JSON array of {message, errorCode(, fields)}; 401 = session ID or OAuth token expired or invalid (INVALID_SESSION_ID, 'Session expired or invalid'); 400 for malformed requests such as bad SOQL

research.language_rationale: The exporter is embedded in an existing .NET data-warehouse loader; the pinned surface is Salesforce's documented raw REST query protocol (opaque cursor URLs, attributes metadata, REST error envelope), and C# with HttpClient plus System.Text.Json exercises that wire contract directly without hiding pagination behind a third-party SDK.

verified_facts: A SOQL query is executed with GET /services/data/vXX.X/query?q=<url-encoded SOQL>; spaces may be encoded as + or %20 and the request carries Authorization: Bearer <token>.

The response JSON carries totalSize (the full result-set count), done (whether the scan is complete), records (the current batch) and — only while done is false — nextRecordsUrl, an opaque server-issued path such as /services/data/v67.0/query/01gRO0000016PIAYA2-500.
Each record embeds an attributes object with the sObject type and the record's resource url alongside the selected fields.
Remaining pages are fetched by requesting nextRecordsUrl exactly as issued (no q parameter, no rebuilding) until a response reports done true; batches default to up to 2,000 records.
REST failures answer a JSON array of {message, errorCode} objects; an expired or invalid session is HTTP 401 with errorCode INVALID_SESSION_ID and message 'Session expired or invalid'.

The single-refresh-per-run session recovery, the retry-once-per-page rule for 503 responses honoring Retry-After via an injected delay, and the requirement that a retried page never duplicates records are local policies pinned in docs/contract.json, not vendor mandates.
